steps.title: Identify the triangle and known sides
steps.content: The figure shows a right triangle with hypotenuse labeled 14 and an acute angle of $58^\\circ$. Segment $AB$ is the side opposite this angle.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$14\\sin 58^\\circ$': Hypotenuse times sine of the given angle gives the opposite side $AB$. ✓
  • Choice B — '$\\frac{14}{\\sin 58^\\circ}$': Would only be correct if 14 were the opposite side and we wanted the hypotenuse. ✗
  • Choice C — '$14\\cos 58^\\circ$': Cosine gives the adjacent side, not the opposite side. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\\frac{14}{\\cos 58^\\circ}$': Would relate the adjacent side to the hypotenuse, not $AB$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
